criterion: Contains no debug prints in new scripts except the player hit placeholder message.

forms.assessment_process: Search new .gd files for print calls and confirm only the hit placeholder remains.
forms.explanation: Stray debug output obscures real errors and makes playtesting unreliable in an existing project.

criterion: Uses a projectile script that moves and frees itself without calling back into the shooter after spawn.

forms.assessment_process: Inspect projectile.gd for movement and queue_free logic, confirm no references to the shooter node after initialization.
forms.explanation: Self-contained projectiles reduce coupling and prevent runtime errors when shooters are freed or disabled.

criterion: Includes one EnemyChaser and one EnemyShooter instance in platformer.tscn, both resting on valid ground.

forms.assessment_process: Open platformer.tscn and verify both instances exist and their collision shapes do not overlap tiles at rest.
forms.explanation: Correct placement ensures the new content is testable in the main level without breaking traversal.

criterion: Prints a "player hit" message when the player collides with the chaser body or is hit by a projectile.

forms.assessment_process: Run the level, touch the chaser, then get hit by a projectile, confirm the message appears in both cases.
forms.explanation: Collision confirmation is the requested placeholder behavior and validates signals and layers are wired correctly.

criterion: Removes each projectile instance on collision with static level geometry.

forms.assessment_process: Fire multiple shots into walls and floors, then inspect the remote scene tree to confirm instances free on impact.
forms.explanation: Cleanup prevents performance degradation and unintended off-screen collisions during longer play sessions.

criterion: Moves each projectile horizontally at a constant speed after spawn, without homing or acceleration.

forms.assessment_process: Record projectile position over time in the debugger and confirm linear motion with constant delta per second.
forms.explanation: Predictable projectile motion is required for fair gameplay and for validating collision timing.

criterion: Uses an exported firing_interval that changes the shooter fire rate when edited per instance.

forms.assessment_process: Edit firing_interval in the inspector for the placed shooter and confirm the time between shots changes accordingly.
forms.explanation: Editor tunables are needed for balancing and for reusing the enemy across multiple levels.

criterion: Maintains the shooter global_position while firing, with no visible movement during normal play.

forms.assessment_process: Run the level for 30 seconds while the shooter fires, confirm its global_position stays constant in the inspector.
forms.explanation: Stationary behavior matches the design and avoids unintended physics drift or recoil bugs.

criterion: Includes exported detection_radius and move_speed on the chaser that are editable per instance in the inspector.

forms.assessment_process: Select the chaser instance and confirm both exported fields appear and affect behavior when changed.
forms.explanation: Per-instance tuning is required for level design iteration without code changes.

criterion: Uses CharacterBody2D and gravity so the chaser stays grounded while chasing on floors and platforms.

forms.assessment_process: Run chase behavior on flat ground and a platform, confirm the body remains on the surface and responds to gravity.
forms.explanation: Physics-consistent movement prevents clipping and ensures the enemy interacts with the level like the player does.

criterion: Stops horizontal movement when the player exits the detection Area2D.

forms.assessment_process: Enter the detection radius to trigger chase, then leave it, confirm velocity.x returns to 0 promptly.
forms.explanation: Reliable disengage behavior prevents enemies from following the player across the whole level unintentionally.

criterion: Moves the chaser toward the player by setting velocity.x sign based on player global_position.x while in range.

forms.assessment_process: Stand left and right of the chaser while in range and confirm it reverses direction to follow player x.
forms.explanation: Correct pursuit logic is the core gameplay behavior and must be deterministic for testing and tuning.

criterion: Maintains zero horizontal velocity for the chaser while the player is outside the detection radius.

forms.assessment_process: Keep the player out of range and confirm the chaser velocity.x stays at 0 and position does not drift.
forms.explanation: Idle stability prevents unintended patrol behavior and makes detection tuning predictable.

criterion: Includes enemy_chaser and enemy_shooter scenes and scripts, plus projectile.tscn and projectile.gd, with matching names.

forms.assessment_process: Check the FileSystem for the named .tscn and .gd files and confirm each scene references its script.
forms.explanation: Consistent naming and separation improves maintainability and matches the requested project conventions.

criterion: Runs the project in Godot 4.5 without new errors introduced by the added enemy and projectile scenes.

forms.assessment_process: Run the project and confirm the debugger shows no errors originating from the new scripts or scenes.
forms.explanation: Build stability is essential in an existing project and prevents regressions during integration.
